name: Citius Pharmaceuticals, Inc.
business: Citius Pharmaceuticals, Inc. is a biopharmaceutical company dedicated to the development and commercialization of first-in-class critical care products, with a focus on oncology, anti-infectives in adjunct cancer care and unique prescription products. In December 2025, the company launched LYMPHIR, an engineered IL-2 diphtheria toxin fusion protein for the treatment of cutaneous T-cell lymphoma. It is also developing Mino-Lok (antibiotic lock solution for catheter-related bloodstream infections), Halo-Lido (topical formulation for hemorrhoids), and NoveCite (mesenchymal stem cell therapy for ARDS).
sellsTo: Oncology patients with cutaneous T-cell lymphoma

exposures.sentence: Citius Pharmaceuticals, Inc., headquartered in Cranford, New Jersey, is a biopharmaceutical company dedicated to the development and commercialization of first-in-class critical care products, with a focus on oncology, anti-infectives in adjunct cancer care and unique prescription products.

catalysts.sentence: In December 2025, we became a commercial company with the launch of LYMPHIR by our majority owned subsidiary Citius Oncology.

catalysts.event: Anticipated preliminary results of Phase I trial of LYMPHIR in combination with CAR-T therapy
catalysts.sentence: Preliminary results are anticipated in the first quarter of 2026.

tamClaims.claim: Addressable U.S. market for LYMPHIR exceeds $400 million
tamClaims.sentence: Based on internal estimates, the Company believes the addressable U.S. market for LYMPHIR exceeds $400 million and may further expand with the introduction of a new therapeutic.

tamClaims.claim: Potential U.S. market for Mino-Lok is approximately $500 million to $1 billion
tamClaims.sentence: We estimate that the potential market for Mino-Lok in the U.S. to be approximately $500 million to $1 billion as shown in the table below based on a target price of up to $400 per dose of each salvage flush treatment.
